title: Shell capacity C(n)=2n^2 vs atomic data

tests.name: C(n)=2n^2 equals sum 2(2l+1) equals tabulated shell capacity
Pure 2n^2 nesting vs measured noble-gas closures
tests.note: Pure 2n^2 nesting matches He(2) and Ne(10) but diverges at Ar(18): measured closures follow the Aufbau/Madelung (n+l) order, not 2n^2. The degeneracy formula is exact; cumulative closure requires the filling rule. Reported as a limitation, not a fit.

tests.name: Madelung (n+l) order reproduces periodic-table row lengths

summary.honest_caveat: C(n)=2n^2 is the exact level degeneracy and is confirmed by subshell sums and tabulated shell capacities; it does NOT by itself give noble-gas closures, which require the n+l filling rule.
